title: Barclays Raises Bloom Energy Stock Price Target on Guidance Boost
text: Barclays raised its Bloom Energy price target to $254, reflecting confidence in the company's raised guidance and expanded AI data center growth thesis. The analyst upgrade came following Bloom's strong Q1 2026 earnings and elevated full-year guidance. The increased price target suggests Barclays sees significant upside from current levels despite the stock's recent outperformance. The upgrade acknowledges the robustness of Bloom's backlog and partnerships with Oracle and Brookfield as de-risking factors for execution. Barclays maintains an Equal Weight rating, suggesting fair valuation relative to peer group despite exceptional growth metrics. The analyst commentary emphasizes the importance of manufacturing capacity expansion and supply chain execution in converting the $20 billion backlog into revenue and profitability. Multiple other analysts including Evercore ISI, BTIG, RBC Capital, and others have similarly raised price targets following strong Q1 results, with consensus price target around $240-$295 range, significantly above current trading levels.
